title: cinnamomum sancti-caroli
intro: Cinnamomum sancti-caroli, also known as the South Indian sandalwood or Santalum carolinum, is a plant species native to Australia and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Santalaceae. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like santalol and sesquiterpenes, with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Cinnamomum sancti-caroli include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and cough. Herbal preparations of Cinnamomum sancti-caroli can be made into teas, infusions, or essential oils, which are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
